parsed_review.summary: CP00-787 (RP25 Migration Platform review risk closeout, Risk C, 86 units, RP25.P09.M06.S07-RP25.P09.M10.S10) passes hardened read-only review. Pack binding is internally consistent: registry.js MIGRATION_PLATFORM_CP787_PACK_BINDING (CP00-787, risk C, 86 units, upstream CP00-786, next CP00-788/RP26.P00.M00.S01) matches the manifest, review-baseline, contract latest_projection, and plan live_state; deliverable distribution counted directly as implementation 57 / claude_review 12 / security_audit 8 / test 4 / ui 4 / hermes_evidence 1 = 86, matching MIGRATION_PLATFORM_CP787_REQUIREMENTS and the prompt baseline. The implementation is descriptor-only: service.js createMigrationPlatformCp787ReviewRiskCloseoutDescriptor and validators.js validateMigrationPlatformCp787ReviewRiskCloseoutDescriptor enforce descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, no source-system runtime opening, no raw/source/credential/real-client payloads, claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false; test/model.test.js asserts the descriptor/packets stay runtime-closed and that the contract projection is latest. Hermes evidence packet is descriptor-only (emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_migration_no_write) and the Claude packet is read_only with promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, so Claude remains an independent read-only reviewer and Hermes is used only for deterministic review-packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. The CP178+ hardening library (scripts/lib/closeout-pack-claude-review-hardening.mjs) correctly rejects auth failures, 0-byte stdout, fenced/malformed JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing required fields, and requires exactly one valid receipt with non-empty stdout, session_id, uuid, numeric cost, empty permission_denials, and zero P0/P1/P2. The pack does not overclaim production readiness: manifest status=in_progress, production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, and adjudication/construction-inspection/claude-review-result are all in the expected pending state; the stricter command-evidence/review cross-checks in validate-closeout-pack.mjs only fire when status=production_ready, so command-evidence.production_ready=true here is correct pre-closeout staging, not a contradiction. Recorded command evidence shows all required validation commands (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) at exit_code 0; per the read-only constraint these were inspected as recorded artifacts and not independently re-executed. Spot-checked contract case-set rows are descriptor-only with all leak-guard flags false (no real client/matter/document/credential/secret data), and no stray or invalid review receipts exist in the artifacts directory. Plan binding is sound via the live-cursor pattern: CP00-787 is the live pack recorded in plan live_state while plan.packs is the forward queue beginning at CP00-788, and the validator's plan_binding_snapshot fallback covers the live pack. No P0/P1/P2/P3 defects found. blocks_goal_closeout is false because this pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust (enterprise_trust_claim_closed and human final approval are recorded as outside this receipt); final human/enterprise-trust approval remains the expected downstream authority boundary and is not a defect of this receipt.
